title: Opinions on Working from Home
transcript: Part 1. You're going to hear five people. You'll hear each person only once. First, read items 41 to 45. After you've listened to each person, decide if the statement for each of the items 41 to 45 is true or not true. Mark your answer on the answer sheet. Now you will have half a minute to read the five statements. Part 2. You're going to hear five people talking about the experience of working from home. Number 41. For me, it has been a fantastic change. I used to spend over an hour every morning just driving to work in heavy traffic. Now I use that time to exercise or have a proper breakfast with my family. I feel much more relaxed when I start my work day. Number 42. I honestly find it quite difficult. I live in a small apartment and I don't have a separate room for an office. I have to work at the kitchen table, which is uncomfortable. Also, my neighbors can be very noisy during the day, so it is hard to concentrate. Number 43. I miss the social side of the job. At the office, we often solved problems just by chatting while getting a coffee. At home, you have to write an email or schedule a call for everything. It feels a bit lonely and the work actually takes longer. Number 44. The biggest benefit is definitely financial. Before, I was spending a large part of my salary on train tickets and buying lunch at restaurants near the office. Now, I cook at home and save hundreds of pounds a month. It makes a big difference to my budget. Number 45. I think the best solution is a mix of both. It is good to stay home when you need to focus on a big project without interruptions. But it is also important to go in once or twice a week to see your team. Companies should offer a hybrid model.

statements.statement: The speaker thinks working from home is less stressful than commuting.
statements.audio_transcript: For me, it has been a fantastic change. I used to spend over an hour every morning just driving to work in heavy traffic. Now I use that time to exercise or have a proper breakfast with my family. I feel much more relaxed when I start my work day.

explanation.de: RICHTIG. Der Sprecher sagt, er habe früher «über eine Stunde im Stau» verbracht und fühle sich jetzt «viel entspannter». Das zeigt, dass Homeoffice für ihn weniger stressig ist als das Pendeln.
explanation.en: CORRECT. The speaker says they used to 'spend over an hour every morning just driving to work in heavy traffic' and now 'feel much more relaxed'. This clearly shows working from home is less stressful than commuting.
explanation.ar: صحيح. يقول المتحدث إنه كان 'يقضي أكثر من ساعة كل صباح في القيادة وسط الزحام' والآن 'يشعر باسترخاء أكبر'. العمل من المنزل أقل إجهاداً بوضوح.
explanation.fr: CORRECT. Le locuteur dit qu'il passait 'plus d'une heure chaque matin dans les embouteillages' et se sent maintenant 'beaucoup plus détendu'. Le télétravail est clairement moins stressant que le trajet.
explanation.es: CORRECTO. El hablante dice que solía 'pasar más de una hora conduciendo en tráfico pesado' y ahora 'se siente mucho más relajado'. Trabajar desde casa es claramente menos estresante que ir al trabajo.
explanation.ru: ПРАВИЛЬНО. Говорящий говорит, что раньше 'проводил более часа каждое утро в пробках' и теперь 'чувствует себя гораздо спокойнее'. Работа из дома явно менее стрессовая.

statements.statement: The speaker has a quiet and comfortable office at home.
statements.audio_transcript: I honestly find it quite difficult. I live in a small apartment and I don't have a separate room for an office. I have to work at the kitchen table, which is uncomfortable. Also, my neighbors can be very noisy during the day, so it is hard to concentrate.

explanation.de: FALSCH. Der Sprecher sagt das Gegenteil: Er arbeite am «Küchentisch», was «unbequem» sei, und seine Nachbarn seien «sehr laut». Er hat also weder einen ruhigen noch einen komfortablen Arbeitsplatz.
explanation.en: INCORRECT. The speaker says the opposite: they work 'at the kitchen table, which is uncomfortable' and their neighbors 'can be very noisy during the day'. They have neither a quiet nor comfortable home office.
explanation.ar: خطأ. يقول المتحدث العكس: يعمل 'على طاولة المطبخ، وهو غير مريح' وجيرانه 'يمكن أن يكونوا صاخبين جداً'. ليس لديه مكتب هادئ أو مريح.
explanation.fr: FAUX. Le locuteur dit le contraire : il travaille 'à la table de la cuisine, ce qui est inconfortable' et ses voisins 'peuvent être très bruyants'. Il n'a ni bureau calme ni confortable.
explanation.es: INCORRECTO. El hablante dice lo contrario: trabaja 'en la mesa de la cocina, lo cual es incómodo' y sus vecinos 'pueden ser muy ruidosos durante el día'. No tiene una oficina tranquila ni cómoda.
explanation.ru: НЕПРАВИЛЬНО. Говорящий говорит обратное: он работает 'за кухонным столом, что неудобно' и соседи 'могут быть очень шумными'. У него нет ни тихого, ни удобного кабинета.

statements.statement: The speaker misses the informal communication with colleagues.
statements.audio_transcript: I miss the social side of the job. At the office, we often solved problems just by chatting while getting a coffee. At home, you have to write an email or schedule a call for everything. It feels a bit lonely and the work actually takes longer.

explanation.de: RICHTIG. Der Sprecher sagt, er vermisse «die soziale Seite der Arbeit». Im Büro hätten sie «Probleme gelöst, indem sie beim Kaffee plauderten». Das ist genau die informelle Kommunikation, die ihm fehlt.
explanation.en: CORRECT. The speaker says 'I miss the social side of the job' and 'at the office, we often solved problems just by chatting while getting a coffee'. This is exactly the informal communication they miss.
explanation.ar: صحيح. يقول المتحدث 'أفتقد الجانب الاجتماعي من العمل' و'في المكتب كنا نحل المشاكل بالدردشة أثناء تناول القهوة'. هذا بالضبط التواصل غير الرسمي الذي يفتقده.
explanation.fr: CORRECT. Le locuteur dit 'je regrette le côté social du travail' et 'au bureau, on résolvait les problèmes en discutant autour d'un café'. C'est exactement la communication informelle qui lui manque.
explanation.es: CORRECTO. El hablante dice 'extraño el lado social del trabajo' y 'en la oficina, solíamos resolver problemas charlando mientras tomábamos un café'. Esto es exactamente la comunicación informal que echa de menos.
explanation.ru: ПРАВИЛЬНО. Говорящий говорит: 'я скучаю по социальной стороне работы' и 'в офисе мы решали проблемы, болтая за кофе'. Это именно то неформальное общение, которого ему не хватает.

statements.statement: The speaker saves money by not traveling to work.
statements.audio_transcript: The biggest benefit is definitely financial. Before, I was spending a large part of my salary on train tickets and buying lunch at restaurants near the office. Now, I cook at home and save hundreds of pounds a month. It makes a big difference to my budget.

explanation.de: RICHTIG. Der Sprecher sagt, der größte Vorteil sei «finanziell». Er habe früher «einen großen Teil seines Gehalts für Fahrkarten» ausgegeben und spare jetzt «Hunderte Pfund im Monat».
explanation.en: CORRECT. The speaker says 'the biggest benefit is definitely financial' and they were 'spending a large part of my salary on train tickets'. Now they 'save hundreds of pounds a month' by not commuting.
explanation.ar: صحيح. يقول المتحدث إن 'أكبر فائدة هي مالية بالتأكيد'. كان 'ينفق جزءاً كبيراً من راتبه على تذاكر القطار' والآن 'يوفر مئات الجنيهات شهرياً'.
explanation.fr: CORRECT. Le locuteur dit que 'le plus grand avantage est financier' et qu'il dépensait 'une grande partie de son salaire en billets de train'. Il 'économise des centaines de livres par mois' en ne se déplaçant plus.
explanation.es: CORRECTO. El hablante dice que 'el mayor beneficio es definitivamente financiero' y gastaba 'una gran parte de su salario en billetes de tren'. Ahora 'ahorra cientos de libras al mes' al no viajar al trabajo.
explanation.ru: ПРАВИЛЬНО. Говорящий говорит, что 'главное преимущество — финансовое'. Он тратил 'большую часть зарплаты на билеты на поезд' и теперь 'экономит сотни фунтов в месяц'.

statements.statement: The speaker believes employees should never go into the office.
statements.audio_transcript: I think the best solution is a mix of both. It is good to stay home when you need to focus on a big project without interruptions. But it is also important to go in once or twice a week to see your team. Companies should offer a hybrid model.

explanation.de: FALSCH. Der Sprecher sagt ausdrücklich, es sei «wichtig, ein- oder zweimal pro Woche reinzugehen» und empfiehlt ein «hybrides Modell». Er meint also nicht, dass man nie ins Büro gehen sollte.
explanation.en: INCORRECT. The speaker explicitly says 'it is also important to go in once or twice a week to see your team' and recommends a 'hybrid model'. They do NOT believe employees should never go into the office.
explanation.ar: خطأ. يقول المتحدث صراحةً إنه 'مهم أيضاً الذهاب مرة أو مرتين أسبوعياً' ويوصي ب~'نموذج هجين'. هو لا يعتقد أنه يجب ألا يذهب الموظفون للمكتب أبداً.
explanation.fr: FAUX. Le locuteur dit explicitement qu''il est important d'y aller une ou deux fois par semaine' et recommande un 'modèle hybride'. Il ne croit PAS que les employés ne devraient jamais aller au bureau.
explanation.es: INCORRECTO. El hablante dice explícitamente que 'también es importante ir una o dos veces por semana' y recomienda un 'modelo híbrido'. NO cree que los empleados nunca deban ir a la oficina.
explanation.ru: НЕПРАВИЛЬНО. Говорящий прямо говорит, что 'важно приходить раз-два в неделю' и рекомендует 'гибридную модель'. Он НЕ считает, что сотрудники никогда не должны ходить в офис.
